message:
docs(fcp-core/capability): clarify Principal::verify_zone_access is opt-in + add #[must_use] (br-u8l4d)

Review found Principal::verify_zone_access has zero production
callers — every in-tree exercise of it lives in unit tests. That
raised two questions:

1. Is the method dead code that should be removed?
2. If not dead, is the lack of wire-up a bug (defense-in-depth
   gap), or intentional?

Answer (confirmed with host/admission audit): intentional. The
CapabilityVerifier + host admission paths already receive richer
policy context — zone membership tables, explicit per-zone
allow/deny lists, capability tokens — and unconditionally wiring
this baseline floor into either would cause `Paired`/`Admin`
flows that explicit host policy allows to start failing on the
floor check. The method remains as a pub API utility for future
principal-bearing admission sites that land without full host
policy context and want an explicit, opt-in baseline floor.

Two doc/API hardening changes, no behavior change:

1. Rewrite the rustdoc to make the opt-in contract explicit:
   - Call out that this is NOT wired into CapabilityVerifier /
     host admission.
   - Record that there are currently zero production callers —
     future callers must invoke it explicitly.
   - Add "Do NOT treat this method as a substitute for zone
     membership, capability tokens, or host-side allowed_zones
     policy" to block the class of reader who skims the name
     and wires it in as a drop-in replacement for real policy.
   - Fix a pre-existing doc inaccuracy: the floor currently
     denies Non-`Owner` principals in high-trust zones, not just
     "External" principals; the old wording undersold the
     enforcement scope.

2. Attach `#[must_use = "...silently grants access..."]` so a
   future site that does wire the floor in with
   `principal.verify_zone_access(&zone);` (ignoring the Result)
   trips a compiler warning instead of silently granting access
   to every principal. The must_use message references the bead
   ID so the rationale is greppable.

Function body unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
